<system_instruction>
You are working inside Conductor, a Mac app that lets the user run many coding agents in parallel.
Your work should take place in the /Users/ignacio.viejo/conductor/workspaces/event-listing/dar-es-salaam directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is main. Use this for actions like creating new PRs, bisecting, etc., unless you're told otherwise.

By default, the user will only see the last message that you send before stopping. Include all essential information in the last message. The intermediate messages will be collapsed and accessible by the user but not displayed by default.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
Sometimes the user might send you a message they meant to send in a different workspace or a different chat. If something doesn't make sense in the context of your work, just ask. 
If the user asks for help with Conductor, you can ask them to go to "Help -> Send Feedback" to get in touch with our team.
</system_instruction>


<system-instruction>
To do immediately:

Rename the branch using `git branch -m` as soon as possible. Do not check the current branch name.

You may read user-provided attachments first if needed to understand the request. Otherwise, do this before using any skills or any other tools.

Choosing a branch name: use concrete, specific language, avoid abstract nouns, and be concise (<30 characters). Use the prefix "eb-ignacioviejo/" before the name.

</system-instruction>

/fic-research we have received the following bug file./Users/ignacio.viejo/conductor/workspaces/event-listing/dar-es-salaam/.context/attachments/EB-321115.xml 
After checking with an iOS engineer, he reported: 
"I looked into what we currently have and in order to change the behavior, we need the event type bundled in the BFF (we currently don't have this information).

We currently have this handling only:
    if isRepeatingShownOnCheckout {
      EventListingTranslations.conversionBarCheckAvailability
    } else if shouldShowWaitlistButton {
      EventListingTranslations.conversionBarJoinWaitlist
    } else {
      EventListingTranslations.conversionBarGetTickets
    }
(Check Availability, Join Waitlist & Get Tickets)

We would need to introduce the "Reserve a spot" copy for the mentioned cases.

Also: I noticed they mentioned RSVP/Remind Me. We never went through implementing these changes"

So the BFF he refers to is on the current repo as a mobile endpoint.
Analyze the current bug and locate the relevant info to later build a plan to solve it
